evidence.text: [E1] Creates file enumeration loop across user directories and reads many documents.
[E2] Writes encrypted copies with new extensions; originals become unreadable.
[E3] Drops ransom note in each directory and changes wallpaper to payment instructions.
[E4] Disables shadow copies / restore points using system commands.
[E5] Spawns process to terminate backup services and database services.
[E1] Mass renames files after rewriting contents with high-entropy bytes.
[E2] Creates mutex to avoid multiple runs and enumerates network shares.
[E3] Attempts to delete backups and disables recovery features.
[E4] Ransom note indicates timed payment and decryption key escrow.
[E1] Reads browser profile databases and extracts saved login records.
[E2] Accesses OS credential store / keychain APIs.
[E3] Compresses stolen data into a single archive for staging.
[E4] Makes outbound POST requests with the archive to a remote endpoint.
[E1] Enumerates installed browsers and reads cookie / session stores.
[E2] Collects autofill data and email addresses from local profiles.
[E3] Targets crypto wallet extensions and copies key material.
[E4] Performs periodic beacon to fetch exfil destination and sends staged bundle.
[E1] Maintains periodic beacon to command endpoint and sleeps with jitter.
[E2] Downloads tasking and executes shell commands received from remote server.
[E3] Creates persistence via scheduled task to relaunch at startup.
[E4] Opens reverse shell when specific command is received.
[E1] Registers as a service and maintains long-lived encrypted channel.
[E2] Receives remote commands and uploads system inventory results.
[E3] Implements keep-alive and retries with fallback domains.
[E1] Reads configuration files and user preferences for an installed application.
[E2] Connects over HTTPS to well-known vendor update servers.
[E3] Downloads signed update package and verifies signature before install.
[E4] Writes logs to application directory; no unusual process injection.
[E1] Performs normal file I/O on user-selected documents; no mass rewrite.
[E2] Uses OS APIs for UI events and stores recent file list.
[E3] Periodic telemetry to vendor with opt-in setting enabled.
[E1] Downloads a second-stage payload and executes it in memory.
[E2] Attempts privilege escalation via known exploit pattern.
[E3] Enumerates processes and injects into a common system process.
[E1] Creates autorun registry entry and hides executable in user profile.
[E2] Connects to unknown domain and fetches encrypted blob.
[E3] Performs environment checks and anti-analysis delays.
